title: <x-subst type="inclFile">
breadcrumbs: XHTML Elements for Static Server Pages
text: <x-subst type="inclFile"> XHTML placeholder that is replaced by the contents of a specified HTML or JavaScript file. <x-subst type = "inclFile" name =" FileName "> Content </x-subst> Arguments FileName —name of the HTML or JavaScript file containing the contents to be substituted. Content —XHTML that does not contain the <x-subst> tags. Description The <x-subst type="inclFile"> element enables external files to be included in the HTML. This can be a file containing standard, reusable content, such as page headers and footers, or sections of JavaScript functionality. You can improve the performance of very large server pages by breaking them up into a number of smaller files. These are then assembled by a master server page which contains only <x-subst> elements for all the files to be included. Related Links Substituting ProcScript Functions and Including Files in Static Server Pages You can use the <x-subst> tag to substitute some ProcScript functions ($formtitle, $putmess and $text) or include files on a static server page. webgen Generate an XHTML document from the layout definition and the component definition of a static server page.
